title: Drone use warning issued by Glasgow Prestwick Airport

sections.heading: What happened
sections.content: Glasgow Prestwick Airport has issued a warning over drone use near the airfield.

sections.heading: Key details
sections.content: The airport warned that using drones close to an airfield could endanger flights (Image: Jason Mavrommatis) This article is brought to you by our exclusive subscriber partnership with our sister title USA Today, and has been written by our American colleagues. It does not necessarily reflect the view of The Herald.

sections.heading: Why it matters
sections.content: Officials say there is a 5km restriction zone around the airport where drones must not be flown due to the potential risk to aircraft operations. The airport warned that using drones close to an airfield could endanger flights and may result in enforcement action.

sourceReferences.citationText: serper-scottish-aviation – Glasgow Prestwick Airport issues important warning over drone use
